description:
You are acting as a junior backup-administrator who has just taken charge of a small archive area located under /home/user/backups.  
 The previous administrator left you three daily “report” files inside /home/user/backups/daily/:

     /home/user/backups/daily/backup_report_2023-08-01.log
     /home/user/backups/daily/backup_report_2023-08-02.log
     /home/user/backups/daily/backup_report_2023-08-03.log

 1. INPUT FILE FORMAT  
    • Each report is plain-text, space-separated, **WITHOUT** a header line.  
    • Every line contains exactly four fields in the order below:  

          FIELD-1 : filename         (string, e.g. db.sql)  
          FIELD-2 : size_kb          (integer, kilobytes)  
          FIELD-3 : checksum         (8-digit hexadecimal)  
          FIELD-4 : date             (YYYY-MM-DD)  

    Example (first two lines of the 2023-08-01 file):
         db.sql 20480 9f8b1e2d 2023-08-01
         home.tar 512000 c3d8fab1 2023-08-01

 2. YOUR GOAL  
    a) Consolidate the three input files into one comma-separated index called  

           /home/user/backups/summary/backup_index.csv  

    b) While building the index you must **re-order** the columns so the final file has the following 4 columns in this exact order:

           date,filename,size_kb,checksum  

    c) Prepend **one single header line** exactly matching the column names above.  
    d) Sort all data lines in ascending order by the date column (YYYY-MM-DD).  
    e) Create a tiny status file  

           /home/user/backups/summary/stats.txt  

       that contains exactly two lines (no extra spaces or blank lines):

           Total_files_processed: 3
           Total_records_indexed: 9

 3. OUTPUT FILE FORMAT SPECIFICATION  
    The automated tester will verify that backup_index.csv is **identical** to the following template:  

        Line-1 (header)   : date,filename,size_kb,checksum  
        Line-2            : 2023-08-01,db.sql,20480,9f8b1e2d  
        Line-3            : 2023-08-01,etc.cfg,120,1a2b3c4d  
        Line-4            : 2023-08-01,home.tar,512000,c3d8fab1  
        Line-5            : 2023-08-02,db.sql,20560,af4c9d11  
        Line-6            : 2023-08-02,etc.cfg,125,ee44aa55  
        Line-7            : 2023-08-02,home.tar,513024,0ddce8ef  
        Line-8            : 2023-08-03,db.sql,20640,bb88cc77  
        Line-9            : 2023-08-03,etc.cfg,130,ddd77711  
        Line-10           : 2023-08-03,home.tar,514048,2376abdc  

    • No trailing whitespace is permitted.  
    • A single newline character must follow every line, including the last one.  

 4. LOGGING REQUIREMENT  
    Besides backup_index.csv and stats.txt, nothing else should be created, moved or deleted.  
    Do **NOT** modify the original log files.  
    Ensure that /home/user/backups/summary/ exists; create it if necessary (you do not have root rights, so stick to the user’s home).  

 5. HINTS (not commands)  
    • Use utilities such as cut and paste (or their POSIX alternatives) to slice and reorder columns.  
    • Use redirection (>) rather than utilities that demand elevated permissions.  
    • Check paths carefully—absolute paths are required.  

 After completing the task successfully, both backup_index.csv and stats.txt must match the exact format described above so that an automated test can verify your work.
truth:
Pre-existing directory structure and file contents before the agent starts:

 1. Directories  
    /home/user/backups/  
    /home/user/backups/daily/  

 2. Files and their **exact** content (each line terminated with a single ‘\n’)  

    /home/user/backups/daily/backup_report_2023-08-01.log  
        db.sql 20480 9f8b1e2d 2023-08-01
        home.tar 512000 c3d8fab1 2023-08-01
        etc.cfg 120 1a2b3c4d 2023-08-01

    /home/user/backups/daily/backup_report_2023-08-02.log  
        db.sql 20560 af4c9d11 2023-08-02
        home.tar 513024 0ddce8ef 2023-08-02
        etc.cfg 125 ee44aa55 2023-08-02

    /home/user/backups/daily/backup_report_2023-08-03.log  
        db.sql 20640 bb88cc77 2023-08-03
        home.tar 514048 2376abdc 2023-08-03
        etc.cfg 130 ddd77711 2023-08-03

 3. Expected artifacts after successful execution:

    a) Directory  
       /home/user/backups/summary/        (permissions 755, owned by user)

    b) /home/user/backups/summary/backup_index.csv  
       (10 lines, each ending with ‘\n’)  
       date,filename,size_kb,checksum
       2023-08-01,db.sql,20480,9f8b1e2d
       2023-08-01,etc.cfg,120,1a2b3c4d
       2023-08-01,home.tar,512000,c3d8fab1
       2023-08-02,db.sql,20560,af4c9d11
       2023-08-02,etc.cfg,125,ee44aa55
       2023-08-02,home.tar,513024,0ddce8ef
       2023-08-03,db.sql,20640,bb88cc77
       2023-08-03,etc.cfg,130,ddd77711
       2023-08-03,home.tar,514048,2376abdc

    c) /home/user/backups/summary/stats.txt  
       Total_files_processed: 3
       Total_records_indexed: 9

 4. No other files should be touched or created.  
    Automated tests will validate both files’ presence and their exact byte-for-byte contents.
